title: Trek Recommendation System
summary: A machine learning-powered web application that recommends hiking and trekking destinations based on similar treks using content-based recommendation algorithms. Originally developed as a freelance project in 2022, open-sourced since. Features a clean search interface, 208+ trekking destinations, and a REST API for programmatic access.

sections.heading: Project Overview
sections.body:
A machine learning-powered web app that recommends trekking destinations based on what you already know you enjoy. You search for any trek from a database of 208+ destinations, and the system returns 10 personalised recommendations ranked by similarity.

Originally developed as a freelance project in March 2022. As of 2024, transitioned to a personal open-source project with full ownership. The codebase has been refactored, documented, and optimised for local development.

sections.heading: How the Recommendation Engine Works
steps: Feature Extraction — Trek descriptions are vectorised using CountVectorizer, converting text into numerical feature vectors.
Similarity Calculation — Cosine similarity is computed between all trek vectors, capturing how similar any two treks are based on description content.
KNN Search — A NearestNeighbors model (scikit-learn) returns the k most similar treks to the query, using the cosine similarity matrix.
Ranking — Results are sorted by similarity distance and returned with trek images, descriptions, type labels, and percentage match scores.
Model Caching — The trained model is serialised to disk (minnn_rf.pkl) and loaded at startup, keeping response times under 1 second.

sections.heading: Core Algorithm
sections.code:
from sklearn.neighbors import NearestNeighbors
from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import CountVectorizer
import pandas as pd
import pickle

# Load trek dataset
df = pd.read_csv('b1.csv')

# Vectorise trek descriptions
vectorizer = CountVectorizer()
vectors = vectorizer.fit_transform(df['description'])

# Fit NearestNeighbors with cosine similarity
model = NearestNeighbors(metric='cosine', algorithm='brute')
model.fit(vectors)

# Get recommendations for a given trek
def recommend(trek_name, n=10):
    idx = df[df['title'] == trek_name].index[0]
    distances, indices = model.kneighbors(
        vectors[idx], n_neighbors=n + 1
    )
    results = []
    for i, dist in zip(indices[0][1:], distances[0][1:]):
        results.append({
            'title': df.iloc[i]['title'],
            'similarity': round((1 - dist) * 100, 2),
            'image': df.iloc[i]['image'],
            'type': df.iloc[i]['treck type']
        })
    return results

Trek images, descriptions, and type labels

sections.heading: Open Source
sections.body: The project is fully open source under the MIT License. Contributions welcome — especially around expanding the trek database, improving the recommendation model, or adding new UI features.
backgrounds: Expanding the trek dataset beyond 208 destinations
Improving the recommendation model (collaborative filtering, hybrid approaches)
Adding user preference inputs to personalise beyond a single trek query
